sheets.notes:
NOT a clean uniform grid, so isGrid=false. It IS a 4-column layout with uniform 180px column pitch (column x-origins: 0, 180, 360, 540), but row heights vary and text-label bands sit between phase groups, so a single cellW/cellH grid would clip wheels or bake in label text. cellW=180 is reliable; there is no single cellH. Rects above are tight content bounding boxes (2px pad) verified by cropping+reading every frame in a montage — all 32 frames are clean, no title/label text baked in, one frame each.

Layout: y22-52 = overall sheet title "ORANGE CAT TREADMILL SEQUENCE" (excluded). Five phase labels sit in-line at the top-left of their first art row (APPROACH ~y88-114, GET ON, RUN, SLOW DOWN, GET OFF). The APPROACH label overlapped col0 fully and bled the letter "H" into col1, so I restricted approach frames 0-1 to y>=130 to exclude it — confirmed clean.

Frame counts: approach 8 (2 rows of 4), getOn 6 (a row of 4 + a row of 2; cols 2-3 of the 2nd getOn row are empty), run 8 (2 rows of 4), slowDown 4, getOff 4. Total 32.

Confidence HIGH on: run (all 8), slowDown, getOff, getOn — verified clean and, for run, uniformly sized (~130x131) and well-registered, which is the important looping cycle. 

Confidence MEDIUM / flags: (1) approach and getOff frames are heterogeneous in size — some are small standalone cats, some are full wheels — because the source art genuinely varies; they will not be pixel-registered to each other, so the app should center each frame in its viewport rather than assume a common cell. (2) A few wheel boxes are wider than 180 due to the 2px pad plus wheels being centered and slightly wider than the column (e.g. approachA2 w=182, approachB1 w=184, getOff1/2 w=184), giving ~2-6px horizontal overlap with the neighboring column; if the slicer needs strictly non-overlapping cells, clamp each to its 180px column (x = col*180, w = 180). (3) getOn frame count = 6, not 8 — verified cols 2-3 of the second getOn row are blank. All view directions are the cat facing right (side view).

High confidence. Sheet is 1440x2976 = exactly 2x the orange wheel sheet, and the underlying structure is a clean 4-col x 8-row grid of 360x372 cells (col x-origins 0/360/720/1080; row y-origins are multiples of 372). Confirmed by per-cell density + crops. Row 3 has only its first 2 cells filled (cols 2,3 are empty). Phase order follows the printed labels top-to-bottom, reading row-major left-to-right: APPROACH = rows 0-1 (8 frames), GET ON = row 2 + row 3 cols 0-1 (6), RUN = rows 4-5 (8), SLOW DOWN = row 6 (4), GET OFF = row 7. Total 31 frames.

I returned TIGHT per-frame bounding boxes (not full 360x372 cells) on purpose: the title 'GREY TABBY TREADMILL SEQUENCE' and each phase label are drawn in the top whitespace of the label rows (0,2,4,6,7), so full-cell slices would bake that text in. The tight boxes exclude all text — verified by cropping and viewing approach r0c0, getOn r3c0, run r4c1, and all 5 getOff frames (each shows one clean cat/wheel frame, no text, no neighbor bleed). If the consumer prefers uniform cells for background-position, use x=col*360, y=row*372, w=360, h=372 — but that WILL include label text on the first cells of label rows.

ONE STRUCTURAL EXCEPTION / flag: GET OFF (row 7) contains 5 frames, not 4 — it does NOT fit the 4-col grid. Frames 0-2 are cat-in-wheel (~240-360px wide, roughly cells 0-2), while frames 3 (leaping cat) and 4 (walking-away cat) are smaller bare-cat sprites packed into the remaining right-hand space and straddle the col-2/col-3 boundary at x=1080. The 5 explicit rects I gave are correct and verified; a naive uniform-cell slicer would merge frame 2's wheel with frame 3's leap in cell 2 and clip frame 3. This is why isGrid is 'true' for the scaffold but GET OFF must use the explicit frame rects.

Camera view is side-on for every sequence (cat runs on the wheel facing right; approach/get-off walking cats also side-profile). All coordinates are in original 1440x2976 sheet pixels.

Sheet is 2692x1568, white background, IRREGULAR (label-grouped rows, not a clean grid). isGrid=false. Content organized as: title band, then per-section label band, then a sprite row. I found content via non-white mask (channels>245 = background), row/column projection into bands, then cropped and Read representative frames per sequence to confirm each rect is ONE clean frame with no baked-in label text and no neighbor bleed.

CONFIDENCE HIGH for: postMainScratch (8), postStopDeparture (5), postSideRight (9), postBackLeft (5), tunnelTopDown (5). These are clean cat+prop frames; coords are tight bounding boxes verified by crop-and-read.

CONFIDENCE MEDIUM / FLAGS:
- postApproachStart: The sheet interleaves the 4 cat frames with EMPTY scratching-post cells (at ~x248-385, 641-777, 1029-1165) and then has 5 more IDENTICAL empty-post cells filling the rest of the row (x~1411-2309). I treated those empty posts as generator filler and OMITTED them, returning only the 4 cat-bearing frames. If you want the empty-post 'rest' cells too, they sit on the same ~190px pitch at y148,h181.
- postMainScratch: a lone fully-scratched post (no cat) sits at x1600-1736,y394 between mainScratch and stopDeparture; I excluded it (transition/damaged-post filler).
- tunnelApproachEnter: mixed — frame1 is the cat ALONE (no tunnel), frames 2 and 4 are EMPTY tunnels, frame3 is the cat entering the tunnel. Tunnel lengths vary (386/567/368 px). I returned all 4 as distinct states in order.
- tunnelHoleInteractions: this row is a clean uniform ~191px pitch, so I emitted it as 14 uniform cells (x0=24, cellW=191, y=1150, h=150). Frame0 = approaching cat, frames1-3 = cat lounging on tunnel, frames4-11 = single tunnels with paw/tail poking through holes, frames12-13 = cat emerging/exiting. The last cell is clipped to w=185 at the sheet's right edge. y-top was pulled to 1150 to exclude the '(Hole Interactions)' label that sits directly above the leftmost sprites; this may clip a few px off the tallest cat-tail tips.
- tunnelTopDown: y-top is per-frame — frames 1-2 (under the 'TOP-DOWN ORTHOGONAL VIEW' label) start at y=1400 to exclude the label text; frames 3-5 start at y=1360 to keep the cats' upward-poking tails. Frame3 is a longer tunnel (w557) with a cat sprawled through it — genuinely one frame, not a merge error.
- 'SIDE LONG-VIEW (Exit)': the sheet HAS this label but I found NO dedicated Exit sprite row beneath it (the Exit label sits directly on top of the Top-Down label/row). The cat-emerging/exit poses appear only as the last 2 cells of tunnelHoleInteractions. So tunnelExit is effectively absent/missing in this sheet — flag for the app author.
- The sheet title text is garbled ('WHITE CA*HELL CAT TUNNEL'), suggesting AI-generated art; this is consistent with the empty-prop filler frames noted above. All returned coords are in ORIGINAL sheet pixels.

Sheet is 1024x596, WHITE background, IRREGULAR (isGrid=false). Content mask = any channel <=245. Method: row-band projection to separate title/label text from frame rows, then per-row column projection (gap=1) to split touching frames, then tight bbox per frame. Verified >=2 crops per sequence at 3x — all show one clean frame, no baked-in label text, no neighbor bleed.

SCRATCHING POST (front-left rows y56-318): approachStart=12 frames — an alternating cat-pose / plain-post sequence (frame1 is cat-only walking-in; frames 8-12 are plain posts, kept in order so timing isn't broken). mainScratch=8 (cat scratching + one trailing plain post at frame8). stopDeparture=6 (cat-only, walking away). sideRight=9 and backLeft=5 both cat+post; split at the label boundary x~670 (side-right label x8-126, back-left label x670-784).

TUNNEL (y378-577): tunnelEnter=4 (cat + 3 long side tunnels; frame3 is legitimately wide at w=217 because the cat pokes out the tunnel's right end). tunnelHoles=13 (cat-only frame1 + 12 tunnels; last frame x888 w=136 is a full-length tunnel with the cat exiting the right end — could arguably belong to the Exit sequence). tunnelTopDown=5 top-down tubes; I subtracted the "TOP-DOWN ORTHOGONAL VIEW" label (y519-531, x9-208) from the mask so frames 1-2 don't grab label text — cat tails that poke above the tubes are preserved (verified).

CONFIDENCE: High on all scratching-post sequences and tunnelEnter/tunnelHoles/tunnelTopDown boundaries (visually verified).

FLAGS:
1) tunnelExit is EMPTY on this sheet — the "SIDE LONG-VIEW (Exit)" label (y500-512) is immediately followed by the "TOP-DOWN ORTHOGONAL VIEW" label with no frame row of its own. No distinct exit-view frames were drawn. Returned with frames:[]. (The full-length tunnel at the end of tunnelHoles, x888, may be the intended "exit" moment placed in the holes row.)
2) tunnelTopDown frame3 (x302,w212) is ~1.5x the others — verified as ONE continuous tube with the cat threading through (tail out top-left, tail/paw out top-right, head out bottom-center). Confident it's a single frame, but flagging the unusual width in case the source intended it as two.
3) approachStart frame heights vary (frame1 h=57 cat-only vs h=70 for post frames) — expected since a frame is the tight bbox of whatever is drawn; if the app needs a uniform cell height use h=71 and a common top of y=56 for that row.
